metadata.title: How to Move a Qubit Without Ever Copying It

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Olá, Liam — in for Bear. Alice holds a quantum state she can't read and can't copy — and she needs to send it to Bob, across the country? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Alice holds a quantum state she can't read and can't copy — and she needs to send it to Bob, across the country?
props.segment: How to Move a Qubit Without

beats.narration_text: Alice holds a quantum state she can't read and can't copy — and she needs to send it to Bob, across the country.

card.copy: How to Move a Qubit Without Ever Copying It

beats.new_visual_element: [TITLE] qubit you can't read, can't copy

beats.narration_text: Measuring would destroy the superposition. Copying is forbidden by quantum mechanics. And there's no quantum channel. So how does the state move?

production_viz.label: Alice and Bob, blocked channel
production_viz.mechanic: Alice circle on left with teal mystery box labeled |ψ⟩; Bob circle on right; crimson X in center (quantum channel blocked); thin slate dashed line at bottom (classical phone). Alice and Bob fade in from their sides; the X draws on last.
production_viz.note: no solution hinted yet — just establish the problem setup

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Alice, mystery qubit, Bob, blocked channel

beats.narration_text: The state can't be measured. It can't be copied. And yet it gets to Bob. How?

card.copy: Can't measure. Can't copy. How does it move?

beats.narration_text: The classical approach: measure the qubit, send the result. But measuring gives one bit — zero or one. Not the two amplitudes that define the state.

production_viz.label: measure gives 1 bit, not the full state
production_viz.mechanic: teal state box on left labeled |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩+β|1⟩; arrow pointing right; gray meter box labeled MEASURE; on the right: large '0 or 1' in slate; below it crimson label 'α, β: gone'. State box slides in from left; arrow Creates; meter FadeIn; output FadeIn with α,β fading to crimson.
production_viz.note: the classical fix fails — establishes why entanglement is the resource

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] measurement trap — one bit, not the state

beats.narration_text: Here's what Alice and Bob did: at some earlier time, they met and distributed a Bell pair. Alice holds qubit A. Bob holds qubit B.

props.headline: Here's what Alice and Bob did: at some earlier time, they…

still.description: Alice and Bob in a quantum optics lab at an earlier time, each holding a small glowing device, a fiber-optic cable connecting their devices — the distribution of a Bell pair
still.prompt: B05, two physicists Alice and Bob in a clean quantum optics laboratory, each holding a small glowing glass sphere or crystal device, an optical fiber cable connecting their two devices, shaking hands in agreement, flat editorial illustration style, cream and teal color palette, dramatic overhead lighting, no text, 16:9 format

beats.narration_text: Neither qubit carries any local information — the Bell pair is maximally entangled. But the correlation between them is a resource. And Alice is about to spend it.

production_viz.label: Bell pair: Alice holds A, Bob holds B
production_viz.mechanic: Alice on left with qubit-A rectangle (teal border); Bob on right with qubit-B rectangle (teal border); a wavy teal line connecting them (entanglement); label |Φ+⟩ below the wavy line; 'Alice' and 'Bob' eyebrow labels in DISPLAY. Qubits slide in from opposite sides; wavy line Creates; label fades in.
production_viz.note: emphasizes that neither half has local info — the resource is relational

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Bell pair — Alice has A, Bob has B

beats.narration_text: The protocol: four steps that move the state without ever reading it.
Alice takes her mystery qubit and her Bell-pair half and runs them through two gates: CNOT, then Hadamard. This sorts the three-qubit state into four branches.

production_viz.label: CNOT + Hadamard sort into 4 branches
production_viz.mechanic: Three horizontal wires (S top, A middle, B bottom — Bob's). A CNOT gate box (slate) straddles S and A wires; an H gate box (slate) on S wire to the right of CNOT. After both gates: four teal arrows branch rightward from the wire pair, labeled 00/01/10/11. Wires Create first; gate boxes FadeIn; branch arrows LaggedStart.
production_viz.note: no amplitude values shown — just the branching structure

beats.narration_text: Alice measures both her qubits, getting one of four outcomes — say, 10. Her qubit collapses. The superposition is gone from her hands.

production_viz.label: Alice measures — mystery state destroyed
production_viz.mechanic: Wires S and A lead into detector rectangles (slate). The mystery qubit teal box ReplacementTransforms to a crimson box labeled 'GONE'. Two classical bit squares (SLATE, labeled '1' and '0') emerge from the detector and slide right. Outcome chip '10' fades in above the classical bits.
production_viz.note: the crimson GONE box is the aha — the original is consumed

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] measurement — teal state → crimson GONE, 2 bits out

beats.narration_text: The state didn't disappear. It landed on Bob's qubit — in one of four scrambled forms.
For outcome 10, Bob's qubit is now Z applied to the original — the state, but with the phase flipped. He holds the right information, but scrambled.

production_viz.label: Bob's qubit: Z|ψ⟩, phase flipped, unreadable
production_viz.mechanic: Bob's qubit rectangle (crimson border) on right, labeled 'Z|ψ⟩' — the state but phase-flipped. A question mark overlaid. A small correction table on left lists four outcomes vs corrections (00→I, 01→X, 10→Z, 11→ZX) — Bob would need to know which row applies. The table and qubit box FadeIn; the question mark grows via animate.scale.
production_viz.note: the scramble is not random noise — it is one of four known unitaries; the only unknown is which

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Bob holds Z|ψ⟩ — scrambled, unreadable without 2 bits

beats.narration_text: Alice phones Bob: two classical bits, 10. Bob looks up the correction: apply Z. He applies it. His qubit becomes exactly the original state.

production_viz.label: 2 bits arrive, Bob applies Z, state restored
production_viz.mechanic: Two slate squares ('1' and '0') travel along a line from left to right toward Bob. Bob's crimson qubit box ReplacementTransforms into a teal box labeled '|ψ⟩ = restored'. A small Z gate icon appears and then dissolves. The teal box is the payoff.
production_viz.note: the teal box is the first time the state returns — emotional payoff of the mechanism

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] 2 bits travel to Bob → Z correction → teal |ψ⟩ restored

beats.narration_text: Before Alice's call, Bob's qubit is completely random — the same maximally mixed state no matter what she measured. The information doesn't arrive until the bits do.

props.headline: Before Alice's call, Bob's qubit is completely random — the same…

still.description: Bob in a lab, holding his qubit device with a static/noise display, his phone on the bench — he's waiting for Alice's call; the qubit shows no information yet
still.prompt: B13, a physicist Bob in a modern quantum computing laboratory, holding a small quantum device with a static noise pattern on its display screen, a telephone on the workbench beside him, a clock on the wall, expression of patient waiting, flat editorial illustration style, cream and slate color palette, no text, 16:9 format

beats.new_visual_element: [STILL · ai] Bob waiting — qubit shows random noise before the call

beats.narration_text: Alice has the plus state: equal 0 and 1. Outcome 10. Bob's qubit becomes minus — phase flipped. He applies Z. Plus state returns. One moved, none copied.

production_viz.label: example: |+⟩ → outcome 10 → |-⟩ → Z → |+⟩
production_viz.mechanic: Three labeled boxes in a horizontal row: Alice '|+⟩' (teal), arrow, Outcome '10' (slate chip), arrow, Bob '|-⟩' (crimson). A Z gate box appears above Bob's qubit, then animates down: Bob's crimson |-⟩ box ReplacementTransforms to teal |+⟩ box. Below: 'one qubit moved · none copied' as a SerifLabel.
production_viz.note: the illustrative worked example — every number is invented, labeled as illustrative in FACTCHECK

beats.narration_text: One Bell pair. Two classical bits. An unknown state moved from Alice to Bob — intact, unread, with no copy left behind.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. Alice has the plus state: equal 0 and 1. Outcome 10. Bob's qubit becomes minus — phase flipped. He applies Z. Plus state returns. One moved, none copied. One Bell pair. Two classical bits. An unknown state moved from Alice to Bob — intact, unread, with no copy left behind.

props.artifactHeading: How to Move a Qubit Without Ever Copying
artifactLines: Before Alice's call, Bob's qubit is completely random — the same maximally mixed state …
Alice has the plus state: equal 0 and 1.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [How to Move a Qubit Without Ever Copying It] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
props.segment: How to Move a Qubit Without

props.title: How to Move a Qubit Without Ever Copying It
props.subline: an unknown state moved from alice to bob — intact, unread, with no copy left beh
